steps.canonical_text: Suspect a heart attack if there is pressure, squeezing, or pain in the center of the chest, especially if it spreads to the arm, jaw, neck, or back or comes with sweating, shortness of breath, or nausea.
Call emergency services now and say you suspect a heart attack so the right team is dispatched.
Have them stop all activity and rest. Sit them down with the back supported, loosen tight clothing, and keep them as calm and still as possible.
If they have prescribed nitroglycerin for chest pain, help them take it exactly as directed on the label.
If emergency services advise it and they are not allergic to aspirin and not bleeding, have them chew one regular adult aspirin slowly.
Stay with them and keep watching breathing and alertness. If they become unresponsive and stop breathing normally, start chest compressions and use an AED if one is available.
